statement: WHEN the PostgreSQL constrained grammar is compiled, THEN the designated entry and retained rules SHALL remain closed, non-empty, terminating, and reachable.
WHEN DISTINCT ON is retained, THEN its expression list SHALL come from the safe identifier-or-constant subset instead of the unrestricted expr_list family.
WHEN ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW is refined, THEN table-only SET EXPRESSION commands SHALL be absent from the materialized-view command family.
WHEN ALTER INDEX is refined, THEN index commands SHALL come from an index-specific command family with bounded statistics values.
WHEN ALTER VIEW is refined, THEN view commands SHALL come from a view-specific command family instead of generic table commands.
WHEN ALTER DOMAIN ADD is refined, THEN only the documented add-constraint family SHALL remain derivable.
WHEN ALTER DATABASE options are refined, THEN the option family SHALL remain inside the engine-valid connection-limit and boolean subset.
WHEN ALTER SEQUENCE table commands are refined, THEN the sequence branch SHALL use sequence-specific command families instead of generic table commands.
WHEN ALTER STATISTICS is refined, THEN statistics values SHALL come from the canonical positive integer subset and exclude DEFAULT.
WHEN ALTER TYPE is refined, THEN type options SHALL come from the documented safe option-name subset and exclude generic operator-definition lists.
WHEN ALTER FUNCTION, PROCEDURE, and ROUTINE are refined, THEN option lists SHALL be generated from a singleton safe subset that excludes duplicate or conflicting combinations.
WHEN PostgreSQL qualified names are canonicalized, THEN they SHALL remain within one-part or schema-qualified two-part depth.
WHEN ALTER ROLE VALID UNTIL is refined for syntax fuzzing, THEN timestamp literals SHALL come from a canonical valid subset.
WHEN GRANT and REVOKE parameter targets are refined, THEN configuration-parameter names SHALL come from a canonical engine-valid subset instead of generic identifier families.
WHEN CREATE ROLE is refined, THEN reserved pseudo-role names SHALL be excluded and the option list SHALL stay inside the singleton safe subset.
WHEN CREATE USER is refined, THEN reserved pseudo-role names SHALL be excluded and the option list SHALL stay inside the singleton safe subset.

WHEN PostgreSQL role membership and drop statements are refined, THEN all role-name positions SHALL come from the canonical safe role-name family instead of generic role lists or pseudo-role specifiers.
WHEN CREATE GROUP is refined, THEN reserved pseudo-role names SHALL be excluded and the option list SHALL stay inside the singleton safe subset.
WHEN ALTER EXTENSION content members are refined, THEN member object targets SHALL stay inside extension-valid object families instead of generic object-name families.
WHEN GRANT and REVOKE large-object targets are refined, THEN large-object OIDs SHALL come from a canonical integer subset instead of generic numeric families.
WHEN CREATE TABLE partitioning is refined, THEN partition strategies SHALL come from the canonical engine keyword subset instead of generic identifiers.
WHEN CREATE TABLE PARTITION OF is refined, THEN that family SHALL exclude temporary table modifiers that conflict with non-temporary schemas.
WHEN temporary relation-creation families are refined, THEN temporary modifiers SHALL bind only to unqualified canonical relation names while non-temporary branches retain qualified names.
WHEN CREATE VIEW explicit columns are refined, THEN the view family SHALL use finite arity select/column families and exclude the unlogged temp modifier.
WHEN INSERT explicit columns are refined, THEN the insert family SHALL use finite arity select/column families instead of raw column-list plus bare SelectStmt combinations.
WHEN INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE is refined, THEN the conflict-update family SHALL require a non-empty inference specification.

WHEN CREATE FUNCTION and PROCEDURE statements are refined, THEN retained routine definitions SHALL include either a canonical SQL language body or a canonical RETURN body instead of an empty definition.
WHEN DROP TYPE and DROP DOMAIN statements are refined, THEN dropped objects SHALL be named by object-name families rather than type-expression families.
WHEN CREATE TEXT SEARCH TEMPLATE is refined, THEN only the documented INIT and LEXIZE option names SHALL remain in that family.
WHEN SELECT set operations are refined, THEN plain SELECT SHALL require a target list and set-operation operands SHALL come from finite equal-arity operand families.
WHEN VALUES clauses are refined for SELECT-family generation, THEN row expressions SHALL come from constant-expression families and multirow clauses SHALL preserve a single finite row arity.
WHEN CREATE OPERATOR is refined, THEN the operator definition SHALL require an explicit procedure and safe type references.
WHEN CREATE AGGREGATE is refined, THEN the aggregate definition SHALL require explicit SFUNC and STYPE options from the safe subset.
WHEN COMMENT ON type-like objects is refined, THEN TYPE and DOMAIN comments SHALL use object-name families and CAST or TRANSFORM comments SHALL use safe type references.
WHEN CREATE CAST is refined, THEN cast source and target types SHALL come from a bounded canonical type subset.
WHEN DROP CAST is refined, THEN cast source and target types SHALL come from the bounded canonical type subset.
WHEN CREATE ASSERTION is refined, THEN CHECK expressions SHALL come from the safe boolean subset instead of the unrestricted a_expr family.
